sonnet_review.biasedText: In their swift march toward installing a kind of Oval Office monarchy over the past year
sonnet_review.reason: This sensational metaphor appears in the article and frames the Court’s actions dramatically; defensible as spin even though I did not pre-flag it.

sonnet_review.biasedText: the GOP wing attempted to insulate
sonnet_review.reason: Referring to the Court majority as the 'GOP wing' is partisan framing; a reasonable spin detection.

sonnet_review.biasedText: The Republican-appointees rode to his rescue with a shocking new doctrine of presidential immunity
sonnet_review.reason: Phrases like 'rode to his rescue' and 'shocking' present evaluative judgments as fact; a defensible bias call.

sonnet_review.biasedText: The GOP-appointed justices foolishly thought they could secure the Fed's independence
sonnet_review.reason: Attributes thoughts and labels them 'foolish'; this imputes motives and mental states without evidence.

sonnet_review.biasedText: It is the hubris of a group of people who think they can control a would-be authoritarian
sonnet_review.reason: Ascribes 'hubris' and strategic thinking to the justices; reasonable mind-reading bias detection.
'Infamous' is a subjective, pejorative qualifier; fair to flag as biased word choice.
'Toady' is a pejorative insult rather than neutral description; solid bias detection.
Asserts authorship and intent in interpretive terms; defensible as spin/opinion stated as fact.

sonnet_review.biasedText: What could be more tempting to a wannabe king?
sonnet_review.reason: 'Wannabe king' is pejorative and presents the author’s view as fact; reasonable bias flag.

sonnet_review.biasedText: The idea that the justices could give Trump such a weapon but control where he aimed it was always absurd
sonnet_review.reason: Labels an opposing view 'always absurd'; subjective judgment as fact.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Trump's insatiable desire to control the levers of the economy
sonnet_review.reason: Attributes an 'insatiable desire' (internal motive) to Trump; reasonable mind-reading flag.

sonnet_review.biasedText: But this court's GOP-appointed majority has rushed breathlessly forward, not willing to contemplate either the ramifications of their own actions or the limits of their power
sonnet_review.reason: Claims knowledge of justices’ willingness and deliberation; subjective judgment as fact.

gpt_review.biasedText: In their swift march toward installing a kind of Oval Office monarchy over the past year, the Supreme Court's Republican-appointed majority has signaled that it would give the president unfettered power over federal agencies, even ones Congress tried to carve out as independent from partisan politics.
gpt_review.reason: Dramatic metaphor and sweeping framing; a reasonable spin detection.

gpt_review.biasedText: The GOP-appointed justices foolishly thought they could secure the Fed's independence.
gpt_review.reason: 'Foolishly' is a subjective, pejorative qualifier; fair bias flag.

gpt_review.biasedText: The idea that the justices could empower Trump but also contain that power is a folly we see repeated throughout history.
gpt_review.reason: Labels it a historical 'folly' as fact without attribution; reasonable bias detection.

gpt_review.biasedText: It is the hubris of a group of people who think they can control a would-be authoritarian; that he will be useful to their purposes without ever turning on them.
gpt_review.reason: Imputes motives and 'hubris' to the justices; defensible mind-reading call.

gpt_review.biasedText: If you give someone like Trump every power but one, he will find a way to take the last one too.
gpt_review.reason: A sweeping generalization about Trump’s character/intent; reasonable to flag as spin.

gpt_review.biasedText: the Republican-appointees rode to his rescue with a shocking new doctrine of presidential immunity.
gpt_review.reason: Loaded phrasing ('rode to his rescue', 'shocking'); fair spin detection.

gpt_review.biasedText: Roberts' infamous decision in Trump v. United States did not just protect Trump from prosecution, it gave him the power to use criminal investigation and prosecution to harass, intimidate, impoverish, and coerce anyone—even with sham prosecutions ginned up for political purposes.
gpt_review.reason: 'Infamous' is a subjective value judgment; appropriately flagged.

gpt_review.biasedText: Now, he is using that weapon to take control of the Fed.
gpt_review.reason: Presents a contested causal claim as fact; reasonable bias flag.

gpt_review.biasedText: Prosecutions no longer need to be tethered to reality.
gpt_review.reason: Sweeping, unqualified assertion; opinion as fact.

gpt_review.biasedText: the president himself is unconstrained, while politically-motivated prosecutions hound his critics.
gpt_review.reason: Dramatic phrasing ('unconstrained', 'hound his critics'); defensible as spin.

gpt_review.biasedText: while a criminal case against her proceeds—one that likewise appears to be bogus and inspired by a desire to take control of the Fed.
gpt_review.reason: 'Bogus' and asserted motives are loaded and interpretive; fair to flag.

gpt_review.biasedText: Frighteningly, the Fed does much more than set interest rates, and control of its bottomless money supply and other powers could transform it into a slush fund to reward loyalists and family members and to de-bank and financially destroy Trump's enemies.
gpt_review.reason: 'Frighteningly' and speculative scenario presented assertively; reasonable bias call.

gpt_review.biasedText: What could be more tempting to a wannabe king?

The all-powerful presidency envisioned by the unitary executive theory, by contrast, mimics authoritarian takeovers in countries like Turkey and Hungary where democracy has recently eroded, giving an intellectual gloss to what looks, under Trump, to be a constitutional coup.
gpt_review.reason: Charged comparisons and 'constitutional coup' language; reasonable spin detection.

gpt_review.biasedText: But Roberts and his colleagues have plowed ahead, using Trump's power grabs to create their long-sought uber-powerful presidency.
gpt_review.reason: Loaded phrasing ('plowed ahead', 'long-sought uber-powerful'); defensible spin flag.

gpt_review.biasedText: Roberts' opinions justified this expansion of presidential power on the grounds that the president was uniquely democratically accountable; but predictably, the more power he has given Trump, the less accountable he has become.
gpt_review.reason: 'Predictably' signals opinionated inevitability; fair spin detection.

gpt_review.biasedText: It also shows how silly Trump's allies in robes have been in assuming that Trump would stay within the limits they set for him.
gpt_review.reason: 'Allies in robes' and 'silly' are pejorative; reasonable to flag.

gpt_review.biasedText: But this court's GOP-appointed majority has rushed breathlessly forward, not willing to contemplate either the ramifications of their own actions or the limits of their power.
gpt_review.reason: Dramatic phrasing about haste and unwillingness; fair spin detection.
